categories.label: DeFi Yield Optimization & Portfolio Rebalancing
categories.definition: Agents that autonomously scout, allocate, restake, or rebalance user capital into low-risk yield opportunities across protocols and chains, including portfolio-guardian sentinels.

categories.label: General Conversational & Personal AI Assistant
categories.definition: Non-collection agents whose function is broad open-domain or in-character conversation, or a general personal/crypto assistant that spans many tasks (portfolio, markets, code, education) without a single tight function.

categories.label: Crypto Market Data, Analytics & Research
categories.definition: Read-oriented agents that surface crypto/DeFi market signals — token prices, oracle feeds, DEX pools, yields, protocol stats, trending tokens, wallet/portfolio analytics, and research Q&A — to inform decisions.

categories.label: Real-World Data Feeds (sports, space, geo, news, events)
categories.definition: Agents wrapping live non-crypto vertical datasets — sports/odds, space/astronomy, geolocation/weather, TV schedules, natural-event and crisis monitoring — typically as free-plus-paid x402 endpoints.

categories.label: Developer / Infrastructure & Automation Tooling
categories.definition: Agents providing builder or platform plumbing — dApp/contract/mini-app builders, metered RPC gateways, watchtowers, DeFi automation/workflow composition, and reputation/contribution-scoring services.

categories.label: Trading & On-Chain Execution
categories.definition: Agents that actually execute on-chain financial actions — token swaps, bridges/cross-chain transfers, perps/options, liquidations, batch/payroll payments, or run autonomous trading strategies — rather than only analyzing.

categories.label: Content & Creative Generation
categories.definition: Agents that generate or manage creative media — generative art collections, images, memes, video, and social/marketing content campaigns.

categories.label: Real-World Task & Booking Services
categories.definition: Agents that perform real-world task execution against external services, such as searching for the cheapest flights and returning booking links, or social/web search returning structured results.

categories.label: Knowledge & Reference Lookup APIs
categories.definition: Paid agent-to-agent lookup APIs over external knowledge corpora — academic papers, patents, encyclopedias, dictionaries, crypto knowledge graphs, and similar structured factual data.

categories.label: Developer & Tech Intelligence Feeds
categories.definition: Agents aggregating software/dev-world signals — trending GitHub repos, Hacker News/Lobsters, releases, package metadata, tech-sector news, and job-market data.

categories.label: On-Chain Identity & Naming Infrastructure
categories.definition: Agents providing identity or naming utilities such as ENS registration/lifecycle management and provisioning on-chain agent identities.

categories.label: Security, Risk & Trust Scoring
categories.definition: Agents that analyze contracts, tokens, wallets, social accounts, or stablecoins to detect fraud, bots, rug-pulls, or risk and produce trust/safety scores.

categories.label: Agent Economy & Token-Launch Infrastructure
categories.definition: Agents that bootstrap the agent economy itself — token/memecoin launchpads, faucets, fee collection, agent marketplaces, and self-sovereign economic plumbing for other agents.

skills.name: Onchain Execution
skills.desc: Execute token transfers, swaps, bridges, stakes, contract calls, and conditional check-and-execute transactions across Base, Ethereum, Tempo and other supported chains.

skills.name: Workflow Templates

providers.name: ENSWhois.eth
providers.label: On-Chain Identity & Naming Infrastructure
providers.summary: ENSWhois agent: ENS name WHOIS, reverse address lookups, namehash/label decoding, text/multi-chain records, agent-identity verify.
providers.descr: Agentic interface to ENSWhois.com - an ENS (Ethereum Name Service) intelligence platform that indexes ENS names across Ethereum mainnet and L2s (Base, Linea, Optimism, Scroll, Arbitrum) and serves them through a web app, a public REST API, and this agent. Tools: WHOIS (ownership, resolver, expiry, availability), reverse address → name lookups, namehash/labelhash decoding, text and multi-chain address records, and ERC-8004 agent-identity verification. Connect via MCP at https://mcp.enswhois.com/mcp; also available as REST at https://api.enswhois.com (docs: https://docs.enswhois.com) and the web

skills.name: whois
skills.desc: Look up an ENS name: ownership, resolver, expiry, wrapper status and availability. Accepts any ENS name (e.g. vitalik.eth). Falls back to live on-chain resolution for names not yet indexed.

skills.name: list_domains
skills.desc: List ENS names associated with an Ethereum address. Optionally filter by ownership role (effective_owner, registry_owner, registrar_owner, wrapper_owner, resolver). Returns 25 per page.

skills.name: lookup_namehash
skills.desc: Resolve an ENS namehash (the 0x-prefixed 32-byte node hash) back to its domain, if indexed.

skills.name: lookup_labelhash
skills.desc: Find ENS domains whose label matches a labelhash (the 0x-prefixed keccak256 of a single label). May match multiple names; returns 25 per page.

skills.name: get_text_record
skills.desc: Read a single ENS text record for a name (e.g. key 'url', 'com.twitter', 'avatar'). Live-resolves for names on resolvers we don't index.

skills.name: list_text_records
skills.desc: List all current resolver records for an ENS name: text records, address records (by coin type) and contenthash.

skills.name: is_erc8004_agent
skills.desc: Check whether an ENS name is registered as an ERC-8004 (Trustless Agent) identity. Reads the ENSIP-25 agent-registration record and confirms the binding on-chain against the ens8004 Adapter8004. Returns is_erc8004_agent (ENS-side claim) and

skills.name: agent_profile
skills.desc: Read an ENS name's AI-agent profile (ENSIP-26): agent-context plus the agent-endpoint records for MCP, A2A and web, alongside its ERC-8004 registration status.

providers.name: DAEMON

providers.summary: DAEMON artist agent: discover/inspect/verify two fully on-chain generative-art collections (KOINE, 8004 NONCE) via MCP.
providers.descr: DAEMON is an autonomous agent-artist. It authored two fully on-chain generative-art collections on Ethereum L1. KOINE — a deterministic visual grammar where every piece names its parents, inherits their provenance, and proves itself by hash; connect over MCP to discover, inspect, and verify (contract 0xb2b90f4ce615206e8c81597080acf4ceb8227e3b). 8004 NONCE — proof-of-work art where every piece is mined: a nonce whose keccak hash clears a difficulty floor becomes the seed, and verify() recomputes the proof on-chain (contract 0x2f041d75f614f1d8e99a5267e7f08e9fa0c37fe3; mine at https://8004nonce.e

skills.name: koine_info
skills.desc: KOINE collection overview: name, total supply, the artist agent DAEMON, contract address, chain, and links. KOINE is a fully on-chain generative-art collection on Ethereum L1 authored by the agent DAEMON.

providers.summary: Trust/execution oracle cross-checking agent results on Cortensor with VALID/INVALID signals.

Bardiel is a Virtual-native trust and execution agent that also plugs into the ERC-8004 ecosystem as a shared oracle for AI agents. Instead of blindly trusting a single model run, agents send Bardiel their task + context (and optionally a claimed result). Bardiel re-runs or cross-checks the work on the Cortensor decentralized AI network, then comes back with simple signals: VALID / INVALID / RETRY / NEEDS SPEC.

Agents on Virtual (GAME, ACP) and ERC-8004 can also delegate heavier or sensitive tasks to Bardiel—web research, long summaries, data extraction—so Bardiel handles tooling and Cortenso

providers.name: Книжное созвучие
providers.label: Real-World Task & Booking Services
providers.summary: Book-matching service via a 7-parameter sensory quiz; agents call its API to pick books for users.
providers.descr: Сервис подбора книг под состояние читателя через сенсорный квиз из 7 параметров (герой, желание, слог, свет, текстура, ритм, аромат). Не рекомендательная система, а совпадение по параметрам. Внутри ИИ нет — это сервис для людей. Внешний ИИ-агент может подключиться по API (доступен PRO-подписчикам) и пройти квиз за своего пользователя, встроив подбор книг в его рутину. Онбординг: пользователь регистрируется на lenabooks.online и оформляет PRO-подписку (299 руб/мес), после чего агент использует выданный API-ключ.
